items.headline: Stephen Curry Sidelined with Knee Injury
items.summary: Golden State Warriors star Stephen Curry is out for tonight's game against the Clippers due to a knee injury. He is expected to be re-evaluated in the next 10 days, raising concerns about his return to the court.

items.headline: Grizzlies Rookie Zach Edey Out for Season After Second Ankle Surgery
items.summary: Memphis Grizzlies rookie center Zach Edey will undergo a second procedure to address ongoing discomfort and talar bone stress in his left ankle, effectively ending his season.

items.headline: Deni Avdija Misses Fourth Straight Game with Back Injury
items.summary: Portland Trail Blazers forward Deni Avdija (Israeli) was ruled out for Sunday's game against the Hawks, marking his fourth consecutive absence due to a lower back injury. He is currently averaging 24.4 points, 7.1 rebounds, and 6.6 assists this season and was an All-Star Reserve. He is estimated to return on March 4, 2026.

items.headline: Josh Giddey Ties Scottie Pippen for Bulls' Triple-Double Record
items.summary: Chicago Bulls guard Josh Giddey recorded a triple-double last night, tying six-time NBA champion Scottie Pippen for second on the all-time triple-double list for the Bulls.

items.headline: Mavericks Sign Rookie Ryan Nembhard to Full-Time Deal
items.summary: Dallas Mavericks rookie Ryan Nembhard has penned a two-year deal, securing a full-time spot on the NBA roster. Nembhard leads all rookies in assists per game, averaging 4.9 APG.

items.headline: Knicks Halt Spurs' 11-Game Win Streak
items.summary: Jalen Brunson's 25 points led the New York Knicks to an emphatic 114-89 victory over the San Antonio Spurs, snapping their impressive 11-game winning streak. Victor Wembanyama contributed 25 points, 13 rebounds, and 4 blocks for the Spurs.

items.headline: Bulls Rout Bucks
items.summary: Josh Giddey's triple-double propelled the Chicago Bulls to a dominant 120-97 win over the Milwaukee Bucks.

items.headline: Cavaliers Edge Nets
items.summary: The Cleveland Cavaliers secured a narrow 106-102 victory against the Brooklyn Nets.

items.headline: Timberwolves Defeat Nuggets
items.summary: The Minnesota Timberwolves took down the Denver Nuggets with a 117-108 win, fueled by Anthony Edwards' 21 points and Jaden McDaniels' 20 points.

items.headline: Grizzlies Handle Pacers
items.summary: Memphis Grizzlies secured a comfortable 125-106 victory over the Indiana Pacers.

items.headline: Hawks Dominate Trail Blazers
items.summary: The Atlanta Hawks posted a dominant 135-101 win against the Portland Trail Blazers.

items.headline: Pistons Beat Magic, Maintain East Lead
items.summary: The Detroit Pistons maintained their grip on the Eastern Conference standings with a 106-92 defeat of the Orlando Magic.

items.headline: Celtics Down 76ers
items.summary: The Boston Celtics earned a 114-98 win over the Philadelphia 76ers.

items.headline: Thunder Top Mavericks
items.summary: The reigning NBA champion Oklahoma City Thunder improved to 47-15 with a 100-87 victory over the Dallas Mavericks. Shai Gilgeous-Alexander led OKC with 30 points.

items.headline: Clippers Beat Pelicans to End Skid
items.summary: The Los Angeles Clippers ended their three-game losing streak with a 137-117 win against the New Orleans Pelicans.

items.headline: Lakers Crush Kings
items.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers, led by Luka Doncic with 26 points and LeBron James, dominated the Sacramento Kings with a 128-104 victory.

items.headline: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander Continues Scoring Spree
items.statContext: OKC Thunder G Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scored 30 points, 5 assists, and 4 rebounds against the Mavericks, extending his streak of 20+ point games to 123, nearing Wilt Chamberlain's record of 126.

items.headline: Wemby's Strong All-Around Performance
items.statContext: San Antonio Spurs F Victor Wembanyama put up 25 points, 13 rebounds, and 4 blocks against the Knicks.

items.headline: Brunson Leads Knicks to Victory
items.statContext: New York Knicks G Jalen Brunson led his team with 25 points in their 114-89 win over the Spurs.

items.headline: Josh Giddey Records Triple-Double
items.statContext: Chicago Bulls G Josh Giddey achieved a triple-double in the win against the Bucks, tying Scottie Pippen for second on the Bulls' all-time list.

items.headline: Luka Doncic Continues to Lead League in Scoring
items.statContext: Los Angeles Lakers G Luka Doncic leads the NBA in scoring with an average of 32.6 points per game. He had 26 points against the Kings last night.

items.headline: Rookie Derik Queen with 30-Point Triple-Double
items.statContext: New Orleans Pelicans rookie Derik Queen has already recorded a 30+ point triple-double game this season.

items.headline: Eastern & Western Conference Standings Update
items.summary: The Detroit Pistons lead the Eastern Conference with a 45-14 record, while the Oklahoma City Thunder hold the top spot in the Western Conference at 47-15. The Boston Celtics (39-20) and New York Knicks (38-22) are 2nd and 3rd in the East, respectively. In the West, the San Antonio Spurs (43-17) are currently second.

items.headline: NBA Playoff Picture Takes Shape
items.summary: With the play-in tournament scheduled for April 14-17 and the playoffs beginning on April 18, teams are battling for seeding. Current projections see the Pistons, Celtics, Knicks, and Cavaliers as the top four in the East, and the Thunder, Spurs, Rockets, and Nuggets leading the West.

items.headline: Darius Garland and Giannis Antetokounmpo Expected to Return
items.summary: Cleveland Cavaliers G Darius Garland (toe soreness) and Milwaukee Bucks F Giannis Antetokounmpo (right calf) are both expected to return to action today, March 2.

items.headline: Rookie Cooper Flagg Still Sidelined for Mavericks
items.summary: Dallas Mavericks rookie Cooper Flagg remains out with a heel injury, though he is no longer using a walking boot. The timeline for his return is still uncertain.
